root_text.an6.66:1.1: “Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.66:1.3: Thinaṁ, middhaṁ, uddhaccaṁ, kukkuccaṁ, assaddhiyaṁ, pamādaṁ—
root_text.an6.66:1.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme appahāya abhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.66:2.1: Cha, bhikkhave, dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.66:2.3: Thinaṁ, middhaṁ, uddhaccaṁ, kukkuccaṁ, assaddhiyaṁ, pamādaṁ—
root_text.an6.66:2.4: ime kho, bhikkhave, cha dhamme pahāya bhabbo arahattaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.66:1.1: “Mendicants, without giving up six things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.66:1.3: Dullness, drowsiness, restlessness, remorse, lack of faith, and negligence.
translation_text.an6.66:1.4: Without giving up these six things you can’t realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.66:2.1: After giving up six things you can realize perfection.
translation_text.an6.66:2.3: Dullness, drowsiness, restlessness, remorse, lack of faith, and negligence.
translation_text.an6.66:2.4: After giving up these six things you can realize perfection.”
